text:
If a person refuses the request of a peace officer to submit to the taking of a specimen, the peace officer shall request the person to sign a statement that:

(1) the officer requested that the person submit to the taking of a specimen;

(2) the person was informed of the consequences of not submitting to the taking of a specimen; and

(3) the person refused to submit to the taking of a specimen.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 724. Implied Consent
##### § 724.031 — Statement Requested on Refusal

If a person refuses the request of a peace officer to submit to the taking of a specimen, the peace officer shall request the person to sign a statement that:

    (1) the officer requested that the person submit to the taking of a specimen;

    (2) the person was informed of the consequences of not submitting to the taking of a specimen; and

    (3) the person refused to submit to the taking of a specimen.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.text: If a person refuses the request of a peace officer to submit to the taking of a specimen, the peace officer shall request the person to sign a statement that:
the officer requested that the person submit to the taking of a specimen;
the person was informed of the consequences of not submitting to the taking of a specimen; and
the person refused to submit to the taking of a specimen.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
